ToolResultBlock(tool_use_id='toolu_0196ZNmn3SNbuWg478NqLzfv', content='I want to build a Financial Stability Board (FSB) website. When someone opens the site, they land on the homepage that displays the latest news and featured reports on financial stability topics. The homepage has a main navigation menu at the top with five primary sections: "ABOUT THE FSB", "WORK OF THE FSB", "PUBLICATIONS", "CONSULTATIONS", and "PRESS".

If I click on "ABOUT THE FSB" in the navigation menu, I'm taken to the About the FSB page that explains the FSB's mandate and mission as an international body that monitors and makes recommendations about the global financial system. This page describes how the FSB promotes international financial stability by coordinating national financial authorities and international standard-setting bodies as they work toward developing strong regulatory, supervisory and other financial sector policies. The page details the mandate of the FSB and the organizational framework including the Plenary, Steering Committee, and three Standing Committees.

From the homepage, if I click on "PUBLICATIONS" in the main menu, it takes me to the Publications page where I can browse through all FSB policy documents, reports, and evaluations. This page shows 914 results with filtering options on the left sidebar. I can filter content by search keyword, content types, policy areas (such as "Climate-related Risks", "Cyber Resilience", "Crypto Assets", and "Nonbank Financial Intermediation"), and by date range using "Published After" and "Published Before" fields. The results are paginated across 92 pages, and each publication listing is clickable and shows the title, date, and a brief description.

If I'm interested in a specific policy area like climate change, I can navigate from the homepage by clicking "WORK OF THE FSB" in the main menu, which takes me to a page showing different policy areas. From there, clicking on "Financial Innovation and Structural Change" under the work areas, or navigating through the breadcrumb path "Work of the FSB > Financial Innovation and Structural Change > Climate-related Risks", takes me to the Climate-related Risks page. This page explains that the FSB is coordinating internationally the work to address climate-related financial risks, and covers four main collapsible sections: Disclosures, Data, Vulnerability Analysis, and Regulatory and supervisory practices and tools. The page also includes a roadmap diagram and access to key documents including progress reports and TCFD Reports.

From the homepage, clicking on "CONSULTATIONS" in the menu takes me to the Consultations page where I can see both current and past public consultations. The page explains that the FSB issues consultations for all policy documents and describes the three transparent steps of the consultation process: consultations generally last 60 days, public responses are published within 15 days of closure, and overview reports are published when final policy documents are approved. The page displays three sections with images: "Current consultations" to find out what the FSB currently consults on, "Past consultations" to browse all public consultations the FSB has conducted, and "Responses to past consultations" where responses received to FSB consultations are available publicly.

If I want to see press materials, I can click "PRESS" in the main navigation menu from any page, which takes me to the Press page. This page organizes 1034 press items including press releases, speeches and statements, and other press materials. The page has filters on the left sidebar including search, policy areas, and date range filters ("Published After" and "Published Before"). The results are paginated with pagination controls at the bottom showing page numbers and a "Next" button, and the latest updates are featured prominently at the top with dates displayed.

All pages include the same main navigation menu at the top with five sections: "ABOUT THE FSB", "WORK OF THE FSB", "PUBLICATIONS", "CONSULTATIONS", and "PRESS", allowing me to navigate between these sections at any time. Each page also includes a breadcrumb navigation below the main menu showing the current page location (for example: "Home > About the FSB" or "Home > Work of the FSB > Financial Innovation and Structural Change > Climate-related Risks"). The homepage also includes three clickable sections at the bottom: "Data", "Video and Audio", and "Organisation and Members", which provide access to additional resources.

About the FSB

The Financial Stability Board (FSB) is an international body that monitors and makes recommendations about the global financial system.

Mandate and Mission

The FSB promotes international financial stability by coordinating national financial authorities and international standard-setting bodies as they work toward developing strong regulatory, supervisory and other financial sector policies. It fosters a level playing field by encouraging coherent implementation of these policies across sectors and jurisdictions.

The FSB, working through its members, seeks to strengthen financial systems and increase the stability of international financial markets. The policies developed in the pursuit of this agenda are implemented by jurisdictions and national authorities.

The Mandate of the FSB

As set out in its Charter, the FSB:

  • Assesses vulnerabilities affecting the global financial system and identifies and reviews regulatory, supervisory and related actions needed to address them.
  • Promotes coordination and information exchange among authorities responsible for financial stability.
  • Monitors and advises on market developments and their implications for regulatory policy.
  • Advises on and monitors best practice in meeting regulatory standards.
  • Undertakes joint strategic reviews of the international standard-setting bodies.
  • Sets guidelines for, and supports the establishment of, supervisory colleges.
  • Supports contingency planning for cross-border crisis management, particularly with respect to systemically important firms.
  • Collaborates with the IMF to conduct Early Warning Exercises.
  • Promotes member jurisdictions’ implementation of agreed commitments, standards and policy recommendations.

Organisational Framework

The FSB operates through its Plenary, Steering Committee, and three Standing Committees. The Plenary is the decision-making body of the FSB and includes representatives of all FSB members.

Plenary

The Plenary takes all decisions of the FSB. It approves the FSB Work Programme, including its strategic direction, and the reports issued by the FSB. Members of the Plenary include senior officials from national authorities — finance ministries, central banks, and supervisory authorities — of 25 jurisdictions, plus international financial institutions and standard-setting bodies.

Steering Committee

The Steering Committee provides operational guidance between Plenary meetings to carry forward the directions of the FSB. Among its tasks are monitoring and guidance of the Standing Committees and reviewing strategic policy issues facing the FSB.

Standing Committees

The FSB conducts much of its work through three Standing Committees:

  • Standing Committee on Assessment of Vulnerabilities (SCAV) — the FSB’s mechanism for identifying and assessing risks to global financial stability.
  • Standing Committee on Supervisory and Regulatory Cooperation (SRC) — addresses coordination issues that arise among supervisors and regulators.
  • Standing Committee on Standards Implementation (SCSI) — promotes FSB member implementation of agreed international standards.

History

The FSB was established in April 2009 as the successor to the Financial Stability Forum (FSF), which had been founded in 1999 by the G7 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors. The FSB Charter, agreed in 2009 and amended in 2012, sets out the FSB’s mandate, structure and processes. The FSB is hosted and funded by the Bank for International Settlements (BIS) in Basel, Switzerland.

Climate-related Risks

The FSB is coordinating internationally the work to address climate-related financial risks.

The FSB published a comprehensive Roadmap for addressing climate-related financial risks. The Roadmap brings together the climate-related work of standard-setting bodies and other international organisations across four interrelated blocks: firm-level disclosures, data, vulnerability analysis, and regulatory and supervisory practices.

The FSB has been working with the International Sustainability Standards Board (ISSB) and other bodies to promote consistent, comparable and reliable climate- related disclosures by financial and non-financial firms. The ISSB’s IFRS S2 standard builds on the recommendations of the Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures (TCFD) and is now the global baseline for climate-related disclosures.

The FSB encourages jurisdictions to use the ISSB standards as a baseline in their regulatory disclosure frameworks and to consider interoperability with other sustainability disclosure regimes.

Reliable, consistent and comparable climate-related data are essential for monitoring and managing climate-related financial risks. The FSB and NGFS have published joint reports on climate-related data gaps and recommendations to address them, focused on improving the availability and reliability of data on physical and transition risks.

The FSB has been assessing climate-related vulnerabilities in the financial system, including through stocktakes of jurisdictional initiatives and analytical work on the potential channels of climate-related risks. Cross-border and cross-sectoral aspects are a particular focus of the FSB’s analytical work.

The FSB works with the standard-setting bodies to develop regulatory and supervisory practices that effectively address climate-related risks across the financial sector, including banking, insurance and asset management. This work covers supervisory expectations, risk management, scenario analysis and stress testing.

Consultations

The FSB issues consultations for all policy documents, ensuring its work benefits from input from stakeholders worldwide.

The transparent FSB consultation process

The FSB is committed to transparency throughout the development of its policy documents. Its consultation process follows three core steps designed to give stakeholders a meaningful opportunity to provide input.

1

60-day consultation

Public consultations generally last 60 days, providing time for stakeholders to consider and respond to proposed policy documents.

2

Responses published

Public responses are published on the FSB website within 15 days of the close of the consultation, unless commenters request confidentiality.

3

Overview reports

Overview reports describing how comments were considered are published when the final policy documents are approved.

ToolUseBlock(id='toolu_01KKhcKwe3ZZsPM8paYvLpQp', name='Write', input={'file_path': '/workspace/README.md', 'content': '# Financial Stability Board (FSB) — Interactive Front-End Prototype

A complete, interactive prototype of the Financial Stability Board (FSB) website built as a single-page React application.

The application implements every feature described in `/workspace/prompt.txt`:

- A homepage with a featured carousel, the latest news, featured reports, and three callouts (Data, Video and Audio, Organisation and Members).
- An About the FSB page describing the FSB’s mandate, mission and organisational framework (Plenary, Steering Committee, three Standing Committees).
- A Work of the FSB page and the drill-down: Work of the FSB → Financial Innovation and Structural Change → Climate-related Risks, which includes four collapsible sections (Disclosures, Data, Vulnerability Analysis, Regulatory and supervisory practices and tools), a roadmap diagram, and key documents including the TCFD report.
- A Publications page with 914 results, left-hand sidebar filters (search, content types, policy areas, date range), and pagination across 92 pages.
- A Consultations page describing the three-step transparent consultation process and the three image-driven sections: Current consultations, Past consultations, and Responses to past consultations.
- A Press page with 1,034 items, sidebar filters (search, policy areas, date range), pagination, and a prominent "latest update" feature at the top.

A consistent main navigation menu with the five required sections (ABOUT THE FSB, WORK OF THE FSB, PUBLICATIONS, CONSULTATIONS, PRESS) and a breadcrumb appear on every page.

---

## Technology Stack

| Layer | Choice |
| --- | --- |
| Framework | React 19 |
| Build tool | Vite 8 |
| Routing | React Router v6 |
| Styling | Hand-crafted CSS (CSS variables, responsive grid) |
| Language | JavaScript (JSX, ES2022) |
| Data | Local mock data generated from real-looking seed lists |

No external UI framework is used so that the design is faithful to the FSB brand language (deep institutional blue, orange accent, serif headings).

ToolUseBlock(id='toolu_01VRJA67t8jiovfqmvffdMK1', name='Write', input={'file_path': '/workspace/DESIGN.md', 'content': '# FSB Website — Design Document

## 1. Goals and Scope

This document describes the design and implementation of an interactive front-end prototype of the **Financial Stability Board (FSB)** website. The prototype implements every requirement enumerated in `/workspace/prompt.txt`, namely a multi-page institutional website with consistent navigation, breadcrumbs, filtering, pagination, and rich content pages.

## 2. Technology Stack

| Concern | Selection | Rationale |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Framework** | React 19 | Mature ecosystem, component reuse, declarative state |
| **Routing** | React Router v6 (`BrowserRouter`) | Standard SPA routing, supports nested layout |
| **Build tool** | Vite 8 | Instant HMR, simple production builds, native ESM |
| **Styling** | Plain CSS with CSS custom properties | No framework lock-in; precise control over the institutional design |
| **Data** | Local JS module (`src/data/mockData.js`) | Fast, deterministic; matches the spec's required counts (914, 1034) |
| **Server (dev)** | Vite dev server on `0.0.0.0:3000` | Matches required URL `http://localhost:3000` |

## 3. Application Architecture

### 3.1 Routing

The application is a single-page application with a shared layout. The layout (`<Layout>`) renders the header, the routed page outlet, and the footer. The following routes are registered in `src/App.jsx`:

| Path | Component | Purpose |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `/` | `Home` | Landing page: carousel, news, featured reports, callouts |
| `/about` | `About` | FSB mandate, mission, organisational framework |
| `/work` | `Work` | Tile grid of policy areas |
| `/work/financial-innovation` | `FinancialInnovation` | Sub-area (drill-down) |
| `/work/financial-innovation/climate-related-risks` | `ClimateRisks` | Detailed page with collapsibles + roadmap |
| `/publications` | `Publications` | 914 items, filters, pagination |
| `/consultations` | `Consultations` | Process explanation + 3 image sections |
| `/press` | `Press` | 1,034 items, filters, pagination |
| `/data` | `DataPage` | Bottom callout target |
| `/video-audio` | `VideoAudio` | Bottom callout target |
| `/organisation` | `Organisation` | Bottom callout target |
| `*` | `Home` | Fallback |

### 3.2 Component Hierarchy

```
<BrowserRouter>
  <Routes>
    <Layout>             ← Header + <Outlet /> + Footer
       ├── Header        ← top bar, brand, search, NAV (5 sections)
       ├── <Outlet>      ← active page component
       └── Footer        ← 4-column footer + bottom row

The active page typically contains:
   Breadcrumb              (always)
   Page hero (h1 + intro)  (most pages)
   Page body               (page-specific)
```

Reusable building blocks (`src/components/`):

- **`Carousel`** — auto-rotating, pause-on-hover, accessible (`aria-roledescription="carousel"`, dots with `role="tab"`).
- **`Breadcrumb`** — receives an array of `{ label, to? }` items and always prepends `Home` (`/`).
- **`Collapsible`** — accessible disclosure widget used by the Climate-related Risks page for its four sections.
- **`Pagination`** — generic pagination with windowed page list, ellipsis logic, and prev/next.

### 3.3 State Management

State is managed locally per page via React `useState` / `useMemo` (filters, pagination, carousel index, accordion open state). No global state is needed because pages do not share user-mutable data; navigation is the only cross-page interaction and is handled by React Router.

### 3.4 Mock Data Strategy

`src/data/mockData.js` exposes:

- 5 carousel slides (using real provided imagery).
- 5 home news articles.
- 3 featured-report sidebar cards.
- 3 home callouts with their target routes.
- **914 publications** (deterministic, seeded) with policy area, content type, date.
- **1,034 press items** (deterministic, seeded) with type, policy area, date.
- 6 top-level work areas and 3 financial-innovation sub-areas.

Generation is deterministic (seeded PRNG) so result counts and ordering are stable on every render.

## 4. Design System

### 4.1 Colour Palette

| Token | Value | Use |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `--fsb-blue` | `#003d6e` | Headings, nav background, brand |
| `--fsb-blue-dark` | `#002a4d` | Top bar, footer |
| `--fsb-blue-light` | `#00558f` | Nav hover state |
| `--fsb-accent` | `#0072bc` | Links |
| `--fsb-orange` | `#e87722` | Nav border, callout tag, accents |
| `--fsb-gray-50…900` | greys | Background, borders, secondary text |

### 4.2 Typography

- **Headings**: `Georgia`, `Times New Roman`, serif — institutional gravitas.
- **Body**: System UI sans-serif stack — performance and legibility.
- Base size 16px with a 1.55 line-height.

### 4.3 Layout Tokens

- Page width capped at 1200px (`.container`) with 24px gutters.
- Two-column page layout (`.two-col`): 280px sidebar + fluid main, collapses below 900px.
- Spacing: 12 / 16 / 20 / 24 / 30 / 36 / 40 / 60 px scale.

### 4.4 Components Visual Patterns

- Sticky filters sidebar on listing pages.
- Coloured icon badges in result lists per content type (`pdf`, `report`, `speech`, `statement`).
- Step-numbered cards on the Consultations page.
- Tile cards with hover lift for work areas / consultations / video & audio.

## 5. Implementation Highlights

- **Image assets** (`/workspace/resources/images/`) are copied verbatim into `app/public/images/` so they're served by Vite as static files.
- **Pagination**: Publications page produces 92 pages out of 914 results at 10 per page — exactly matching the prompt.
- **Filtering**: search is matched against title and description; date range uses native `<input type="date">`; checkbox groups for content types and policy areas. A "Clear filters" button resets all state.
- **Accessibility**: every interactive element has labels / `aria-*` attributes; breadcrumb is wrapped in `<nav>` with `aria-current="page"` on the active leaf; carousel exposes the right roles.
- **Responsive**: grid layouts collapse to single column at ≤ 900px; the navigation wraps; the search box wraps below the brand.
- **Routing UX**: a `useEffect` in `Layout` scrolls to top on every route change.

## 6. Deployment

`/workspace/start.sh` is a self-contained Bash script:

1. Installs Node.js 20.x via the NodeSource repository if Node ≥ 18 is not already present (`apt-get` based).
2. Installs project dependencies with `npm install` (if `node_modules` is absent).
3. Starts the Vite dev server with `--host 0.0.0.0 --port 3000`.

This was verified end-to-end by deleting `node_modules`, running `bash /workspace/start.sh`, and confirming an `HTTP 200` response on every route at `http://localhost:3000`.

## 7. Future Extensions

While outside this prototype's scope, the structure supports:

- Replacing mock data with a real REST/GraphQL backend (just swap `mockData.js` for fetch calls).
- Adding URL-driven filter state via `useSearchParams` so filters are shareable.
- Internationalisation through `react-intl` (FSB documents are typically English-only, but the building blocks are in place).
- Server-side rendering by porting to Vite SSR or Next.js once content is data-driven.

result:
The FSB website prototype is fully built, deployed, and verified. Summary of deliverables in `/workspace`:

**Source code & app** — `/workspace/app/` (Vite + React 19 + React Router):
- `src/App.jsx` — route table for all 11 pages
- `src/components/` — Layout, Header (with 5-item main nav), Footer, Breadcrumb, Carousel, Collapsible, Pagination
- `src/pages/` — Home, About, Work, FinancialInnovation, ClimateRisks, Publications, Consultations, Press, DataPage, VideoAudio, Organisation
- `src/data/mockData.js` — 914 publications, 1,034 press items, work areas, callouts, slides
- `src/index.css` — full FSB design system (institutional blue + orange accent, serif headings)
- `public/images/` — all 67 provided images copied for static serving

**Documentation**:
- `/workspace/README.md` — overview, tech stack, directory structure, deployment, feature list
- `/workspace/DESIGN.md` — architecture, routing, component hierarchy, design tokens, implementation notes

**Deployment script**:
- `/workspace/start.sh` — self-contained: installs Node.js 20 if missing, runs `npm install`, starts Vite on `http://localhost:3000` with `--host 0.0.0.0 --port 3000`

**Verification** — after `rm -rf node_modules package-lock.json && bash /workspace/start.sh`, every route (Home, About, Work, FinancialInnovation, ClimateRisks, Publications, Consultations, Press, Data, VideoAudio, Organisation) returns HTTP 200, and all images load correctly.

**Implemented features (one-to-one with the prompt)**:
- Homepage with carousel, news, featured reports, and three bottom callouts (Data / Video and Audio / Organisation and Members).
- About the FSB with mandate, mission, Plenary + Steering Committee + 3 Standing Committees.
- Work of the FSB → Financial Innovation and Structural Change → Climate-related Risks with 4 collapsible sections (Disclosures, Data, Vulnerability Analysis, Regulatory & supervisory practices) + roadmap image + key docs incl. TCFD report.
- Publications page showing 914 results, sidebar filters (search, content type, policy areas, Published After/Before), and 92-page pagination.
- Consultations page with 3-step transparent process (60 days / 15 days / overview reports) and three image sections.
- Press page with 1,034 items, sidebar filters, pagination, and prominent "Latest update" feature at top.
- Persistent top nav with 5 sections and breadcrumb on every page.
